steps.title: Identify the chronological progression
steps.content: The passage moves through time: Shakespeare arrived in 1585, was becoming known by 1593, and in 1600 would create another masterpiece. The blank must signal movement forward from 1593 to 1600.

steps.title: Match the transition type
steps.content:
The logical relationship is sequence in time: the passage moves from 1593 (Shakespeare becoming known) to 1600 (another achievement). The blank needs a transition signaling forward movement through time.
  • Choice A — Conversely,: signals direct opposition, so fails — the 1600 achievement does not contradict the 1593 one. ✗
  • Choice B — Later,: signals subsequent time, so fits — 1600 comes after 1593 and "Later" marks that forward progression. ✓
  • Choice C — Thus,: signals logical consequence or cause-and-effect, so fails — delighting audiences in 1600 is not caused by becoming known in 1593. ✗
  • Choice D — In other words,: signals restatement or clarification, so fails — the 1600 sentence is a new event, not a rephrasing. ✗
That is the correct answer.
